ai_summary: Broad short pressure across 10 high-pressure stocks with 100% confirmation via above-average turnover indicates systematic selling rather than isolated positioning; SamsungElec's dominant 4.15 score suggests concentration risk in a single mega-cap name.

Score-weighted portfolio return of -2.24% matched by price declines across the top 5 names (range: -1.19% to -2.62%) confirms short pressure is translating directly into realized losses with no divergence.
Zero unconfirmed shorts at elevated pressure levels signals shorts are not ahead of price action; current sellers have already moved the market, reducing near-term squeeze vulnerability.
Foreign and institutional net outflows are weighted components in the scoring model, indicating coordinated liquidation pressure rather than retail-driven short accumulation alone.
